meta.purpose: Map GrantPro sections to real federal portal form fields for guided submission feature

steps.description: Search for the funding opportunity on Grants.gov by CFDA number, keyword, or agency.
Click 'Apply' on the grant opportunity page. Enter an Application Filing Name (your project workspace name), then click 'Create Workspace'.
On the Manage Workspace page, click 'Participants' tab. Add members by Grants.gov username or email. Set access level: 'All Forms including Budget' or 'Non-Budget Forms'.
Complete the SF-424 form FIRST. Workspace auto-populates certain fields on other forms from SF-424 data, saving time and avoiding typos.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro provides all data needed for SF-424 fields. User copies from GrantPro output.

steps.description: Complete all other required forms in the package. Forms can be filled online (Webform) or downloaded as PDFs, filled offline, and re-uploaded.
Upload narrative attachments, budget justification, and other required documents as PDF files to the appropriate form fields.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates all narrative PDFs and budget documents ready for upload.

steps.description: Click 'Check Application' button. Every form must reach 'Passed' status. System alerts you to missing required fields via pop-up.
After all forms show 'Passed' status, the Authorized Organization Representative (AOR) clicks 'Sign and Submit'. SAM.gov registration must be active.
steps.grantpro_note: Only the AOR can submit. GrantPro reminds user to verify SAM.gov is active.

sf424_form_fields.description: Standard Form 424 - Application for Federal Assistance. This is the cover page for most federal grant applications.
pre_populated_from_sam: Legal Name of Applicant (from SAM.gov registration)
UEI (Unique Entity Identifier, assigned by SAM.gov)

fields.field: 3. Date Received by State / State Application Identifier

subfields: Address (Street 1, Street 2, City, County, State, Province, Country, ZIP/Postal Code)
Person to be contacted (Prefix, First Name, Middle Name, Last Name, Suffix, Title, Phone, Fax, Email)

options: Public/State Controlled Institution of Higher Education

fields.note: Catalog of Federal Domestic Assistance number, pre-filled from opportunity

fields.field: 16. Is Application Subject to Review by State EO 12372?
options: No, program not selected by state

common_forms_in_package.note: Legal certifications, user must review and sign

file_requirements.max_package_size: 200 MB total for entire application package
file_requirements.accepted_formats: Varies by agency; most require PDF for narratives
file_requirements.filename_allowed_chars: A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore, hyphen, space, period, parenthesis, curly braces, square brackets, ampersand, tilde, exclamation point, comma, semicolon, apostrophe, at sign, number sign, dollar sign, percent sign, plus sign, equal sign
file_requirements.rejection_risk: Duplicate filenames or invalid characters can cause entire application rejection

gotchas: SAM.gov registration must be active at time of submission (not just initiated)
SAM.gov registration can take 7-10+ business days; renew well before deadline
Fill SF-424 FIRST - it auto-populates fields on other forms
All forms must show 'Passed' status before Submit button is available
Only the AOR (Authorized Organization Representative) can submit
Filename duplicates across attachments can cause rejection
File naming characters are strictly enforced - no special unicode
Workspace allows multiple team members to edit different forms simultaneously
Forms can be filled online (Webform) OR downloaded as PDF, filled offline, and re-uploaded
Check the 'Form State' field under Applicant & Workspace Details - must read 'No Errors'

used_by: Office of Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS)

steps.description: Locate the Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) in JustGrants. The Entity Administrator must assign the application to an Application Submitter.
Fill in organizational details, project title, award amounts (total, federal, match). Must align with budget section.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro provides all org info and budget totals to paste here.

steps.description: Navigate to 'Proposal Abstract' section. Enter/paste text directly into the text box. This is a web form field, not a file upload.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates the abstract. User copies and pastes into the JustGrants text box.

steps.description: Fill in any data tables or specific data points requested by the NOFO (varies by program).
Upload the proposal narrative as a PDF attachment. Typical sections: Description of Need, Project Goals & Objectives, Project Design & Implementation, Capabilities & Competencies.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates the full narrative PDF ready for upload.

steps.description: For OJP awards: use the WEB-BASED budget form (mandatory for new competitive awards). Enter line items in each category. For COPS/OVW: may use attachment upload instead.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro provides line-item budget data organized by category. User enters values into web form.

steps.description: Upload MOUs, letters of support, resumes, indirect cost rate agreements, and other supporting documents.
Review and certify standard federal assurances and disclosures.
Review entire application, then certify and submit. The Entity Administrator or authorized submitter completes final submission.

application_sections.note: Budget totals HERE must match the Budget Detail section exactly

fields: Proposal Abstract text (paste into text box)
application_sections.note: This is a TEXT ENTRY field, not a file upload. Copy/paste from GrantPro.

fields: Varies by NOFO - performance measures, demographic data, etc.

typical_subsections: Description of the Issue / Statement of Need
Plan for Collecting the Data Required for Performance Measures
application_sections.note: Upload as single PDF. Check NOFO for page limits.
OJP requires web-based form for new competitive awards. Budget can be year-by-year or lump-sum per NOFO instructions.
Justification for each line item. May be part of the web-based form or a separate upload.

common_attachments: Research and Evaluation Independence and Integrity Statement

application_sections.note: Legal certifications. User must review and check each assurance.

file_requirements.accepted_formats: PDF for narratives; varies for other attachments
file_requirements.note: Duplicate filenames are rejected by the system

gotchas: Entity Administrator must assign application to an Application Submitter before work can begin
Budget totals in 'Standard Applicant Information' MUST match Budget Detail section exactly
OJP requires web-based budget form (not attachment) for new competitive awards
COPS and OVW may use different budget entry methods than OJP
Proposal Abstract is a TEXT BOX, not a file upload - copy/paste only
Year-by-year vs lump-sum budget depends on the specific NOFO
JustGrants has frequent maintenance windows - don't wait until deadline day
File names limited to 59 characters, must be unique
Required fields are marked with asterisk (*) - complete all before clicking Continue
After completing each section, click 'Continue' to advance (changes are saved)
The submitted Proposal Abstract is pulled forward to the Funding Recommendation - make it strong

steps.description: Login to Research.gov. From the Proposal Preparation landing page, select the proposal submission type from the drop-down (e.g., Full Proposal, Preliminary Proposal, Letter of Intent).
Complete the setup wizard which collects basic proposal information: program solicitation number, proposal type, PI information.

Fill in the web form with program announcement number, project title, duration, primary place of performance, human subjects/vertebrate animals involvement, international activities, tribal impact, etc.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro provides project title, duration, performance site info.

steps.description: Upload or enter the Project Summary (1 page). Must include: Overview, Intellectual Merit, Broader Impacts as separate sections.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates Project Summary with all three required subsections.

steps.description: Upload the Project Description as a PDF (15-page limit unless solicitation says otherwise). This is the main narrative.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates the full Project Description PDF.

steps.description: Upload bibliography as a PDF. No page limit. Must include all references cited in the proposal.
Enter budget information in the web-based budget form. Categories: Senior Personnel, Other Personnel, Fringe Benefits, Equipment, Travel, Participant Support, Other Direct Costs, Indirect Costs.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro provides line-item budget organized by NSF categories.

steps.description: Upload budget justification as PDF (max 5 pages). Explains and justifies each budget item.

steps.action: Upload Facilities, Equipment, and Other Resources
steps.description: Upload description of available facilities and resources as PDF.
Upload biosketches for all senior personnel. NSF uses SciENcv format.
steps.grantpro_note: User must generate from SciENcv; GrantPro can advise on content.

steps.description: Upload Data Management Plan, Mentoring Plan (if applicable), Letters of Collaboration, etc.
Research.gov runs automated PAPPG compliance checks. Errors block submission; warnings allow it. The Sponsored Projects Office (SPO) at your institution typically does final submission.

proposal_sections.note: This is a multi-page web form, not a file upload
All three subsections are REQUIRED and must be clearly labeled
This is the main proposal narrative. Upload as PDF.
Bibliography of all works cited in the proposal

categories: B. Other Personnel (Postdocs, Grad Students, Undergrads, Secretarial, Other)
F. Participant Support Costs (Stipends, Travel, Subsistence, Other)
G. Other Direct Costs (Materials/Supplies, Publication Costs, Consultant Services, Computer Services, Subawards, Other)
proposal_sections.note: Entered into Research.gov web form. Year-by-year entry.
Describe labs, equipment, computing, office space available for the project
Required if postdocs are included in the budget
Brief, factual commitment letters only. NOT letters of support/endorsement.

formatting_requirements.compliance_check_note: Errors block submission; warnings allow submission but should be addressed

gotchas: Project Summary MUST have three clearly labeled sections: Overview, Intellectual Merit, Broader Impacts
Project Description is strictly 15 pages - system enforces this
Budget Justification is strictly 5 pages
Font requirements are enforced by automated compliance checking
1-inch margins are checked automatically
Biographical Sketches must use SciENcv (mandatory as of recent PAPPG updates)
Letters of Collaboration must be factual only - do NOT include advocacy or endorsement language
The SPO (Sponsored Projects Office) typically does final submission, not the PI
Research.gov may auto-convert uploaded Word docs to PDF, but results can be unpredictable - upload PDF directly
Compliance check errors will BLOCK submission - resolve all before deadline
Data Management Plan is required for all proposals
NSF uses 'Intellectual Merit' and 'Broader Impacts' as review criteria - address both prominently

steps.description: Login to ASSIST with eRA Commons credentials. Search for the Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA). Enter project title and PI's eRA Commons User Name. System pre-populates institutional info from eRA Commons registration.
Fill in the cover page form. Many fields are pre-populated from eRA Commons profile. This is a web-based form in ASSIST (mirrors the old PDF form).
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro provides project title, dates, funding amounts, PI info.

steps.description: Web form with additional NIH-specific fields: human subjects, vertebrate animals, stem cells, inventions, etc.
Web form with yes/no questions plus PDF attachment uploads for: Project Summary/Abstract, Project Narrative (public health relevance), Facilities & Other Resources, Equipment.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates Project Summary, Project Narrative, and Facilities documents as PDFs.

steps.description: Web form listing all performance sites for the project.
Web form for each key person. Upload Biosketch (PDF) and Current & Pending Support for each person. Some fields pre-populated from SF424.
steps.grantpro_note: Biosketches must be generated via SciENcv (mandatory as of Jan 2026). GrantPro can advise on content.

steps.description: Choose EITHER R&R Budget (detailed, for requests >$250K/year) OR PHS 398 Modular Budget (for requests up to $250K/year in direct costs). Web-based forms with some attachment uploads.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates budget data in the correct format for either budget type.

steps.description: This is where the science goes. Upload PDF attachments for: Introduction (resubmissions only), Specific Aims (1 page), Research Strategy (6-12 pages depending on mechanism), and other required/optional sections.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro generates Specific Aims and Research Strategy as PDFs ready for upload.

steps.description: Run system and business validations. All errors must be resolved. Application status must reach 'Ready for Submission'.
The institutional Signing Official (SO) submits the application. PI cannot self-submit.
2 business day window to review assembled application in eRA Commons. Can 'reject' to pull back and fix errors, then resubmit before deadline.
steps.grantpro_note: GrantPro reminds users about the 2-day viewing window.

assist_form_tabs.description: ASSIST presents forms as tabs across the top of the interface, mirroring the old SF424 PDF form pages.

key_fields: PI Contact Information (pre-populated from Commons profile)
Estimated Project Funding (Total Cost, Direct Costs, Indirect Costs)
Human Subjects (yes/no, IRB details, clinical trial info)

pdf_uploads.description: 30-line summary of proposed activity. Include public health relevance.
2-3 sentence description of relevance to public health. Displayed on NIH RePORTER.
Description of scientific environment and resources.
List of major equipment available for the project.

pdf_uploads_per_person.note: SciENcv is MANDATORY as of January 25, 2026. Do NOT flatten the PDF.

forms.when_to_use: Requests exceeding $250,000 in direct costs in any single year
categories_per_year: A. Senior/Key Person (Name, Role, Calendar/Academic/Summer Months, Requested Salary, Fringe)
B. Other Personnel (Postdocs, Grad Students, Undergrads, Secretarial, Other - Number, Calendar Months, Requested Salary, Fringe)
E. Participant/Trainee Support Costs (Tuition, Stipends, Travel, Subsistence, Other)
F. Other Direct Costs (Materials & Supplies, Publication Costs, Consultant Services, ADP/Computer, Subaward/Consortium Costs, Equipment Rental, Alterations & Renovations, Other 1-6)
I. Total Direct and Indirect Costs

forms.when_to_use: Requests of $250,000 or less in direct costs per year
fields: F&A Costs per period (Rate, Base, Amount)

forms.description: This is where ALL the science narrative goes. Every section is uploaded as a separate PDF.

attachments.note: THE most important page of the application. Must include long-term goal, hypothesis, specific aims, and expected outcomes.

subsections: Approach (how you will do it, including preliminary data)

attachments.attachment: Authentication of Key Biological and/or Chemical Resources

pdf_requirements.format: PDF only for all narrative attachments
pdf_requirements.must_not_be_fillable: Use non-fillable PDFs. Do NOT use fillable PDF forms.
pdf_requirements.biosketch_format: SciENcv MANDATORY as of January 25, 2026. Do NOT flatten the PDF.
pdf_requirements.font_size: 11pt or larger (Arial, Helvetica, Palatino Linotype, Georgia). Smaller fonts OK for figures/tables.
pdf_requirements.margins: At least 0.5 inches on all sides
pdf_requirements.line_spacing: No more than 6 lines per inch
pdf_requirements.color: Color images allowed but application is reviewed in grayscale by some reviewers
pdf_requirements.hyperlinks: Allowed but reviewers are not required to follow them

gotchas: PI CANNOT submit - the institutional Signing Official (SO) must submit
eRA Commons account required for PI and all senior/key personnel
SciENcv is MANDATORY for biosketches as of January 2026 - do NOT flatten the PDF
Use non-fillable PDFs only - fillable PDF forms will cause errors
Choose EITHER R&R Budget OR Modular Budget, NEVER both
Modular Budget = $250K or less in direct costs per year (in $25K modules)
2 business day viewing window after submission - check assembled application carefully
Can 'reject' application during viewing window to fix errors and resubmit (if before deadline)
Filename max 50 characters including .pdf extension
Application must pass all validations to reach 'Ready for Submission' status
Specific Aims is uploaded as its own separate PDF (1 page)
Research Strategy is uploaded as its own separate PDF (page limit varies by mechanism)
Each section of the Research Plan is a SEPARATE PDF upload, not one combined document
PI's eRA Commons User Name entered during initiation pre-populates institutional info
Color figures may not render well for reviewers who print in grayscale

grantpro_section_to_portal_mapping.description: Maps GrantPro-generated content sections to where they go in each portal

mappings.grants_gov: Upload as PDF to 'Project Narrative Attachment Form' or paste into SF-424 abstract field
mappings.justgrants: PASTE into 'Proposal Abstract' TEXT BOX (not upload)
mappings.research_gov: Upload as PDF or enter in 'Project Summary' section (must include Overview, Intellectual Merit, Broader Impacts)
mappings.era_assist: Upload as PDF in 'R&R Other Project Information' > 'Project Summary/Abstract' attachment slot

mappings.grants_gov: Include in Project Narrative PDF upload
mappings.justgrants: Include in Proposal Narrative PDF upload (as 'Description of the Issue')
mappings.research_gov: Include in Project Description PDF upload
mappings.era_assist: Include in Research Strategy PDF upload (under 'Significance')

mappings.grants_gov: Include in Project Narrative PDF upload
mappings.justgrants: Include in Proposal Narrative PDF upload (as 'Project Goals and Objectives')
mappings.research_gov: Include in Project Description PDF upload
mappings.era_assist: Include in Specific Aims PDF upload

mappings.grants_gov: Include in Project Narrative PDF upload
mappings.justgrants: Include in Proposal Narrative PDF upload (as 'Project Design and Implementation')
mappings.research_gov: Include in Project Description PDF upload
mappings.era_assist: Include in Research Strategy PDF upload (under 'Approach')

mappings.grants_gov: Include in Project Narrative PDF upload
mappings.justgrants: Include in Proposal Narrative PDF upload (as 'Capabilities and Competencies')
mappings.research_gov: Upload as 'Facilities, Equipment, and Other Resources' PDF
mappings.era_assist: Upload as 'Facilities & Other Resources' PDF in R&R Other Project Information

mappings.grants_gov: Enter in SF-424A form (web form or PDF) with line items by category
mappings.justgrants: Enter in WEB-BASED budget form (OJP) with line items in categories A-J
mappings.research_gov: Enter in Research.gov budget web form by category (Senior Personnel through Indirect Costs)
mappings.era_assist: Enter in R&R Budget web form (detailed, >$250K/yr) or PHS 398 Modular Budget web form (<=250K/yr)

mappings.grants_gov: Upload as PDF to 'Budget Narrative Attachment Form'
mappings.justgrants: May be part of web-based budget form or uploaded as PDF attachment
mappings.era_assist: Upload as PDF in the Budget form's 'Budget Justification' attachment slot

mappings.grants_gov: Include in Project Narrative PDF upload
mappings.justgrants: Include in Proposal Narrative PDF (as 'Plan for Collecting Performance Measures Data')
mappings.research_gov: Include in Project Description PDF upload
mappings.era_assist: Not a standard NIH section; include in Research Strategy if relevant

mappings.grants_gov: Include in Project Narrative PDF upload
mappings.justgrants: Include in Proposal Narrative PDF upload
mappings.research_gov: Include in Project Description PDF upload if solicitation requires
mappings.era_assist: Not a standard NIH section; include if FOA requires

mappings.grants_gov: Upload as PDF(s) to 'Other Attachments Form'
mappings.research_gov: Upload as 'Letters of Collaboration' (factual commitment only, no advocacy language)
mappings.era_assist: Upload in 'PHS 398 Research Plan' or as supplementary attachment

mappings.grants_gov: Upload as PDF attachment if required by agency
mappings.research_gov: Upload as 'Data Management Plan' PDF (max 2 pages, required for all proposals)
mappings.era_assist: Upload as 'Resource Sharing Plan' PDF in PHS 398 Research Plan

guided_submission_instructions.description: Templates for the guided_submission.html feature showing users exactly what to do
grants_gov_checklist: 1. Verify your SAM.gov registration is ACTIVE (check at sam.gov > Entity Registrations)
2. Go to Grants.gov and search for your opportunity number
5. Open the SF-424 form FIRST and fill in all fields (use GrantPro org profile data)
6. Fill in remaining forms using GrantPro-generated data
7. For each narrative attachment: click the attachment field > Browse > select the GrantPro-generated PDF
8. IMPORTANT: Ensure each uploaded file has a UNIQUE name (max 50 characters)
9. Click 'Check Application' - resolve any errors until all forms show 'Passed'
10. Have your AOR click 'Sign and Submit'
justgrants_checklist: 1. Ensure your Entity Administrator has assigned the application to you
3. Complete 'Standard Applicant Information' (use GrantPro org profile and budget totals)
4. Navigate to 'Proposal Abstract' > PASTE your GrantPro-generated abstract into the text box
5. Complete 'Data Requested with Application' per NOFO instructions
6. Navigate to 'Proposal Narrative' > Upload your GrantPro-generated narrative PDF
7. Navigate to 'Budget Detail' > Enter line items from GrantPro budget into the web form (categories A-J)
8. CRITICAL: Verify budget totals match between Standard Applicant Information and Budget Detail
9. Upload all additional attachments (MOUs, letters, resumes, indirect cost agreement)
10. Complete 'Disclosures and Assurances' (review and check each box)
12. Note: Files max 25 MB each, filenames max 59 characters, no duplicate names
research_gov_checklist: 1. Login to Research.gov > Proposals > Prepare New
2. Select submission type from dropdown (e.g., Full Proposal)
3. Complete the Proposal Setup Wizard (solicitation #, proposal type, PI info)
4. Complete the Cover Sheet web form (project title, duration, performance site)
5. Enter/upload Project Summary with THREE required sections: Overview, Intellectual Merit, Broader Impacts
6. Upload Project Description PDF (max 15 pages) - generated by GrantPro
8. Complete Budget web form (year-by-year, all NSF categories)
9. Upload Budget Justification PDF (max 5 pages) - generated by GrantPro
10. Upload Facilities, Equipment, and Other Resources PDF
11. Upload Biographical Sketches (MUST be from SciENcv)
12. Upload Current and Pending Support (MUST be from SciENcv)
13. Upload Data Management Plan PDF (max 2 pages)
14. Upload any additional required documents per solicitation
15. Run compliance check - resolve ALL errors before submission
16. Submit through your institution's SPO (PI typically cannot self-submit)
17. FORMATTING: PDF, approved fonts (Arial 10pt+, TNR 11pt+), 1-inch margins, single-spaced
era_assist_checklist: 1. Verify all senior/key personnel have eRA Commons accounts
2. Login to ASSIST (public.era.nih.gov/assist)
3. Search for your FOA number
4. Enter project title and PI's eRA Commons User Name (triggers institutional pre-population)
5. Complete SF424 (R&R) tab (web form - most org fields pre-populated)
6. Complete PHS 398 Cover Page Supplement tab (human subjects, animals, etc.)
7. Complete R&R Other Project Information tab:
- Upload Project Summary/Abstract PDF (max 30 lines)
- Upload Project Narrative (2-3 sentences, public health relevance)
- Upload Facilities & Other Resources PDF
- Upload Equipment PDF
8. Complete Project/Performance Site Locations tab
9. Complete R&R Senior/Key Person Profile tab:
- Upload Biosketch PDF per person (SciENcv MANDATORY, do NOT flatten)
- Upload Current & Pending Support per person
10. Complete Budget tab (R&R Budget if >$250K/yr OR Modular if <=$250K/yr, NEVER both)
- Upload Budget Justification PDF
11. Complete PHS 398 Research Plan tab - upload EACH section as SEPARATE PDF:
- Specific Aims (1 page) - generated by GrantPro
- Research Strategy (6-12 pages depending on mechanism) - generated by GrantPro
- Additional sections as required by FOA
12. Run validation - resolve all errors until status = 'Ready for Submission'
13. Notify your institutional Signing Official to submit
14. After submission: CHECK your assembled application within the 2 business day viewing window
15. If errors found: 'Reject' in eRA Commons, fix, and resubmit before deadline
16. PDF RULES: Non-fillable only, 50-char filename max, 11pt+ font, 0.5-inch margins minimum
